cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Smarsh Research: 55% of Enterprises Deploy AI but Only 26% Have Governance Keeping Pace
stories.plain: A large-scale 2026 study finds that most enterprises are racing to deploy AI while governance frameworks lag far behind — only 26% say controls are keeping pace. This matters for anyone buying or building marketing AI because the compliance and security risks from ungoverned deployments are growing fast and will likely drive new regulatory and procurement requirements.

stories.head: HubSpot May 2026 Updates: Breeze AI Becomes an Active Workflow Participant, Not Just a Prompt Tool
stories.plain: HubSpot's May release pushes its Breeze AI from a passive assistant into an agentic participant that acts inside workflows — plus smarter automation and data-cleaning tools. For marketers evaluating CRM-adjacent platforms, this signals a real shift in how a major CEP vendor is embedding AI into everyday operations, changing what you should compare when buying.

stories.head: How AI Discovery Is Changing the Metrics Marketers Should Track
stories.plain: As AI-powered search engines surface answers directly, traffic-based metrics lose meaning and marketers need new ways to prove performance. The piece explains what to measure instead — shifting the conversation from page visits to brand presence inside AI answers. Essential reading for anyone building or funding a content or SEO strategy right now.

stories.head: Scrunch Platform Demo: How Brands Get Cited by ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Claude
stories.plain: A hands-on walkthrough of Scrunch, a platform built specifically for generative engine optimization, shows marketers how to track and improve brand visibility inside AI answer engines like ChatGPT and Perplexity. As AI search displaces traditional SEO, tools like this represent a new category of martech spend that buyers need to understand.

stories.head: Stensul Releases Governed Creation Platform with Figma, Salesforce MC Next, and AI Personalization Controls
stories.plain: Stensul's major update adds integrations with Figma, WRITER, and Salesforce Marketing Cloud Next while embedding governance controls directly into AI-era content creation. This means enterprise teams can let AI generate campaign content without bypassing approval workflows — a practical solution to the brand-safety and compliance gap that holds many companies back from scaling AI content.

stories.head: Smarsh Research: 55% of Enterprises Deploy AI but Only 26% Say Governance Keeps Pace
stories.plain: A new study of enterprise AI adoption finds a stark gap: more than half of companies are running AI in production, but fewer than one in three say their governance frameworks are keeping up. That mismatch creates real compliance and security exposure — directly relevant to anyone buying or building marketing AI right now.

stories.head: Salesforce Agentforce Commerce Hits GA; Amazon's Creative Agent Now Produces 80% of an Ad
stories.plain: Two platform milestones in one day: Salesforce Agentforce Commerce reaches general availability, putting agentic shopping automation in front of enterprise buyers, while Amazon says its Creative Agent now generates 80% of ad creative autonomously. Both moves signal agentic AI shifting from pilot to mainstream in commerce and advertising.

stories.head: How AI Discovery Is Changing Every Metric Marketers Measure
stories.plain: As consumers find brands through ChatGPT and Perplexity rather than Google, traffic-based metrics are losing meaning. The article walks through what to measure instead — citation share, AI answer presence, and downstream conversion — giving practitioners a practical framework for adapting performance reporting in the generative-engine era.

stories.head: Stensul Releases Governed Creation Platform with Figma, Salesforce Marketing Cloud Next, and AI Personalization Controls
stories.plain: Stensul's update threads AI content creation directly into approval workflows — connecting Figma design, WRITER copy generation, and Salesforce Marketing Cloud Next in one governed pipeline. The key shift: AI-generated content can't go live without built-in compliance checkpoints, addressing the control gap that has made brand and legal teams wary of generative tools.

stories.head: HubSpot May 2026 Updates: Breeze AI Becomes an Active Workflow Participant, Not Just a Prompt Tool
stories.plain: HubSpot's May release moves its Breeze AI layer from reactive assistant to proactive workflow agent — taking actions across CRM, email, and data-cleansing tasks without a user prompt at each step. For teams evaluating all-in-one platforms, this update changes what HubSpot can own end-to-end versus what still needs a specialist tool.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: AI Search Is Rewriting Brand Visibility Rules
body: When AI assistants answer queries directly, the click-through metrics marketers have relied on for two decades stop working. Share of voice in AI-generated answers is now a distinct metric from search ranking, and it is determined largely by what third-party sources write about a brand — not by the brand's own content.

Platforms like Scrunch are emerging specifically to measure AI visibility, signalling that an entirely new category of MarTech is forming. Marketers who delay building an AI-search strategy are effectively ceding discovery to competitors who publish more credible, citation-worthy content.
ev: Agile Brand · Scrunch AI visibility platform
MarTech · how AI discovery changes measurement

themes.head: AI Governance Gap Threatening Enterprise Adoption
body: Smarsh research and Heimdal analysis both confirm the same uncomfortable finding: enterprises are shipping AI capabilities faster than they can build the controls to manage them. The deployment speed is commercially motivated, but the governance deficit creates regulatory, reputational, and data-security exposure that leadership is only beginning to quantify.

Stensul's new Governed Creation™ release is a direct product response to this gap, introducing structured guardrails for AI-generated marketing content. The decision now facing CMOs is whether to slow adoption to match governance maturity or invest urgently in oversight tooling — because doing neither is no longer a defensible position.
ev: Agile Brand · Heimdal AI risk management

themes.head: Agentic Workflows Move from Pilot to Production
body: Multiple items this month — from HubSpot's Breeze expansion to Hermes Agent-style workflow guides to Agile Brand's agentic transformation expert session — signal that AI agents are graduating from experimentation into mainstream marketing operations. The shift is architectural: agents don't just automate tasks, they orchestrate sequences of decisions across tools, fundamentally changing how teams structure their work.

Workato's 99% Gartner peer-recommendation score and HubSpot's May update bundle reflect customer demand for reliable, production-grade orchestration, not just flashy demos. Teams that have mapped their workflows to agent handoffs are reporting measurable efficiency gains, making this a competitive wedge rather than a future aspiration.
ev: MarTech · 3 AI agents for marketing workflows
Agile Brand · From personalization to agentic transformation

themes.head: Fragmented Stacks Face a Consolidation Reckoning
body: Martech's hidden cost is no longer theoretical: siloed point solutions create data inconsistency, duplicated spend, and integration debt that compounds as AI layers are added on top. The fragmented stack amplifies AI errors because bad data in one tool propagates through every connected agent or model downstream.

The Audyence–Demandbase native integration and Treasure AI's CDP leadership recognition both illustrate the counter-trend: buyers are rewarding platforms that reduce integration surface area rather than adding new connectors. The strategic decision is whether to consolidate proactively or wait for a data-quality crisis to force the issue.
ev: MarTech · hidden cost of fragmented stack
Agile Brand · Treasure AI CDP leadership
Agile Brand · Audyence & Demandbase native integration

themes.head: Predictive Creative Intelligence Before Campaigns Launch
body: Zappi's Amplify AI, xpln.ai's Ideal Attention Time, and Guideline's Verified Ad Intelligence all launched within the same news cycle, pointing to a convergent bet: AI can reliably predict creative performance before a single impression is served. This collapses the traditional test-and-learn loop from weeks into hours and shifts budget decisions upstream.

The implication for marketing teams is structural — creative, media planning, and analytics roles that used to operate sequentially now need to work in parallel against a shared predictive signal. Organisations that adopt pre-flight intelligence gain a compounding advantage because every campaign generates training data that sharpens the next prediction.
ev: MarTech Series · Zappi Amplify AI predictive testing
MarTech Series · xpln.ai Ideal Attention Time
MarTech Series · Guideline Verified Ad Intelligence

month.takeaway: Across every layer of the stack this month, the common pressure is the same: AI is forcing marketers to make foundational architectural decisions — about governance, measurement, data unity, and workflow design — that can no longer be deferred without ceding competitive ground.
